title: selliguea hirtella
intro: Selliguea hirtella, also known as the Polypodium hirtellum or Mountain Fern, is a type of tropical fern native to the Philippines and other parts of Southeast Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to boost the immune system and reduce stress. In herbal preparations, Selliguea hirtella is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also sometimes combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
